content.full_text:
August 1, 1990

Jeanne Pritchard, Division Chief
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Pritchard,

I'm writing to request an opinion regarding my officeholder account.

This account is carrying a loan made to another elected official before Proposition 73 took effect. I'm hoping to have a zero bank account by the time of the November election and to be able to close that campaign committee. How should I handle the money owed by the elected official to that committee? Do I have to have it repaid in order to completely close that committee or can I forgive it?

I would appreciate hearing your response in the near future.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
BILL LOCKYER
State Senator

BL:pdj
